summary: Iteration 3 frontend E2E re-verification of the super_admin role self-heal fix on /hrms.html. ALL 7 review steps PASS. (1) BUG-FIX VERIFIED: After login with admin@iaad.gov.in / Admin@12345, #user-role badge reads 'super_admin' (was 'user' in iteration_2). The self-heal merge-update on onAuthStateChanged + handleLogin works correctly against the pre-existing stale doc. (2) Sidebar nav-users AND nav-auditLogs are both rendered. (3) Users page loads, [data-testid='add-user-btn'] visible at top-right, table lists 2 rows (admin@iaad.gov.in + munendra.sharma31@gmail.com, both super_admin Active). (4) Audit Logs page loads, id=dt-audit table is present and renders 79 log entries including the just-completed Login event with role=super_admin. (5) REGRESSION#1 PASS: forgot-password empty-email .app-toast 'Enter your email first' appears within 1s. (6) REGRESSION#2 PASS: User dropdown → #logout-btn returns to login screen. (7) REGRESSION#3 PASS: Employees page loads, Add modal opens, DOB 1980-06-15 → retirementDate auto-calc = 2040-06-30 (correct).

integration_issues.flow: DataTables on Users / Audit Logs pages
integration_issues.issue: Repeated console PAGE ERROR: '$(...).DataTable is not a function' fires every time Users or Audit Logs is opened. The pages still render correctly because the code falls back to a plain HTML table, but the jQuery DataTables plugin used by initDt() is not loaded (missing script tag or load-order race). No user-visible breakage but search/sort/pagination features the code presumably intends are not active.

verifications_passed: BUG-FIX (HIGH): #user-role badge reads 'super_admin' on login as admin@iaad.gov.in (self-heal of stale 'user' doc works on every login)
Users page: data-testid='add-user-btn' visible top-right; DataTable lists 2 rows (admin + Super Admin), both with role=super_admin badge and Active status
Audit Logs page: id=dt-audit table renders 79 entries; the just-completed login is logged with role=super_admin
REGRESSION#1: forgot-password empty-email .app-toast 'Enter your email first' appears
REGRESSION#2: User dropdown → #logout-btn returns to login screen
REGRESSION#3: Employees page loads, Add modal opens, retirement auto-calc DOB 1980-06-15 → 2040-06-30
action_items: Optional: include the jQuery DataTables plugin (or guard initDt() with a typeof $.fn.DataTable === 'function' check) to silence the repeated 'DataTable is not a function' console errors on Users and Audit Logs pages. Functionality is not blocked but errors are noisy and lose sort/search/pagination.
critical_code_review_comments: Self-heal fix is correct but runs unconditionally on EVERY auth state change → an extra Firestore write per login for the seed admin. Cheap, but worth noting. Could short-circuit with `if (snap.data().role !== 'super_admin')` (which appears to be the case).
index.html still ~1900+ lines — flagged in previous iterations; consider splitting into modules.
DataTables initialization fails silently in the catch path. Better to detect missing plugin once at bootstrap and warn the developer with a clearer message.
test_credentials: admin@iaad.gov.in / Admin@12345 — login successful, now correctly provisioned with role='super_admin'.
seed_data_creation: No new data created. Used the existing TEST001 Test Employee record from iteration_2. The Add Employee modal was opened only to verify retirement auto-calc, then cancelled without saving.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Role-elevation HIGH bug from iteration_2 is FIXED and verified. Only a non-blocking observation remains: jQuery DataTables plugin is not loaded on Users / Audit Logs pages (console errors '$(...).DataTable is not a function'), but tables render with fallback HTML. Bullet-proof on the requested scope; no further re-test needed unless DataTables enhancement is requested.
rca_of_the_issue: Fixed in iteration_3: handleLogin and auth.onAuthStateChanged now (a) force role='super_admin' on doc-create when the email is admin@iaad.gov.in and (b) self-heal an existing users/{uid} doc whose role !== 'super_admin' via merge-update. Verified live: top-right badge reads 'super_admin' immediately after login, and the audit-log entry for the current login records role=super_admin. Older audit entries from iteration_2 still show role=user, confirming the prior bad state has been healed forward.
